steps.title: Find the time signal
steps.content: The sentence reads: "Currently, the frog's range ______ parts of northern New South Wales and southeastern Queensland." The adverb "Currently" signals the present moment — what the range includes right now.

steps.title: Match verb tense to the signal
steps.content: Rule: time signals lock the required tense. "Currently" requires simple present tense. A. "had included" — past perfect. Fails: past perfect describes something completed before another past event, not the current situation. B. "will have included" — future perfect. Fails: describes something that will be completed at some future point, contradicting "Currently." C. "includes" — simple present tense. Directly matches "Currently" to describe the range as it exists now. Correct. D. "included" — simple past. Fails: describes what was true at a past time, not the present state signaled by "Currently." That matches choice C — C is correct.
